Reducing rmin and the bond length shrinks the effective molecular size, further compacting the liquid. Raising the bond force constant stabilises the shorter bond and limits vibrational amplitude. Introducing opposite partial charges on the BCO and MS2 sites creates a permanent dipole; larger charge magnitude yields a dipole closer to the experimental 3.96 D. Together these changes target the observed deficiencies (low density, low ΔHvap, zero dipole) while keeping surface tension near its current good value.

The control fork provides a baseline to isolate the impact of each modification.
